evals.name: list-issues
evals.prompt: Can you check what issues are tracked for the plugin-ops plugin? I want to see what's open before I start working on it. The plugin source is at /Users/ivintik/dev/personal/tools/claude-plugin-ops
evals.expected_output: Reads ISSUES.md and displays a summary table of issues with ID, status, severity, and title. If no ISSUES.md exists, offers to initialize one.

assertions.description: Attempts to read ISSUES.md at the plugin root, or checks if it exists

evals.name: add-issue
evals.prompt: While auditing the plugin-ops plugin at /Users/ivintik/dev/personal/tools/claude-plugin-ops, I noticed that the optimize skill doesn't handle plugins with no skills directory — it crashes with a KeyError. Log this as a bug.
evals.expected_output: Adds a new issue to ISSUES.md with title about optimize skill crashing on missing skills dir, severity major or critical, and correct ISSUE-NNN format.

assertions.description: Writes a new issue entry to ISSUES.md (creates file if missing)
Issue has Status, Severity, Found date, Description fields in ISSUE-NNN format
Does not ask user for fields already clear from the prompt (title and description are inferable)

evals.name: resolve-issue
evals.prompt: I just fixed ISSUE-001 in the plugin-ops plugin — the reflect skill was skipping hooks when the hooks dir didn't exist. I added a guard for that. Mark it resolved. Plugin is at /Users/ivintik/dev/personal/tools/claude-plugin-ops
evals.expected_output: Finds ISSUE-001 in ISSUES.md, updates status to resolved, sets resolved date, and records the resolution description from the user's message.

assertions.description: Locates and reads ISSUE-001 from ISSUES.md
Sets Resolution field with description of the fix
